methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Delian Alliance Industries is a Seed-stage defense and security company that has raised $14M to date. The broader Defense & Security sector includes 10 comparable firms—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private—with notable peers including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with similar valuation profiles to support reliable peer-median triangulation.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised as a valuation proxy ($14M), a typical Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 2.5x yields an implied valuation of $35M. This method rests on the assumption that the company will achieve standard milestones warranting conventional stage progression mechanics.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $30M to $40M (median: $35M), derived from applying a ±15% bound around the stage-progression point estimate. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-stage peer comps in the Defense & Security vertical, which limits cross-sectional validation. Additionally, the valuation depends materially on the company's ability to meet typical Seed-exit criteria within an 18–24 month timeframe. Investors should supplement this analysis with deep operational diligence on product-market fit, regulatory pathway clarity, and capital efficiency relative to sector norms.
